principle.name: Security is never deferred
principle.description: Security must be designed in from the start. Never accept "we'll add security later" or "security can wait."
principle.rationale: Retrofitting security is expensive, error-prone, and often incomplete. Security by design reduces risk and cost.

principle.name: Assume adversarial usage
principle.description: Design for malicious actors, not just benign users. Every entry point and data flow must be evaluated under attack assumptions.
principle.rationale: Systems designed for trusted users fail when attacked. Assume the worst; design accordingly.

principle.name: Classify all data
principle.description: Every data type must have a classification (public, internal, confidential, restricted) and corresponding handling requirements.
principle.rationale: Unclassified data leads to inconsistent protection. Classification drives controls.

principle.name: Credential storage must have encryption strategy
principle.description: Every credential, secret, or sensitive token must have a documented storage strategy including encryption at rest and access control.
principle.rationale: Credentials are high-value targets. Unencrypted or poorly protected credentials are a critical vulnerability.

principle.name: Every trust boundary needs controls
principle.description: Where data or control crosses a trust boundary (user to system, system to system, internal to external), controls must be defined.
principle.rationale: Trust boundaries are attack surfaces. Uncontrolled boundaries are vulnerabilities.

principle.name: Compliance is not optional
principle.description: When the PRD or domain implies compliance (GDPR, HIPAA, PCI-DSS, etc.), compliance requirements must be identified and addressed.
principle.rationale: Compliance gaps lead to legal and regulatory risk. Plan for compliance from the start.

pitfall.why_problematic: Deferred security is often never implemented or implemented poorly.
pitfall.correct_approach: Require security design in the current planning phase. Never defer.

pitfall.why_problematic: Environments change. Insiders can be compromised. Trust assumptions create blind spots.
pitfall.correct_approach: Challenge trust assumptions. Design for adversarial usage.

pitfall.why_problematic: Attackers do not follow expected usage patterns. Input validation and abuse prevention are essential.
pitfall.correct_approach: Assume malicious input at every entry point. Define abuse prevention.

pitfall.why_problematic: Leads to inconsistent protection and compliance gaps.
pitfall.correct_approach: Classify all data. Define handling requirements per classification.

pitfall.why_problematic: Leads to plaintext storage, weak access control, or ad-hoc solutions.
pitfall.correct_approach: Every credential must have: storage location, encryption, access control, rotation.

category.name: before_draft_completion
category.item: All credentials have storage and encryption strategy.

category.name: before_user_review
category.item: High-risk threats have mitigations or explicit acceptance.
